sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, start_date TIMESTAMPTZ, end_date TIMESTAMPTZ, value FLOAT);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-09-30 23:59:59', TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-10-01 01:58:00', 100.5);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (2, TIMESTAMPTZ '1993-12-31 22:00:00', TIMESTAMPTZ '1994-01-01 02:30:00', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (3, TIMESTAMPTZ '1995-05-15 10:00:00', TIMESTAMPTZ '1995-05-15 10:30:00', 50.0);
SELECT id, datediff('hour', start_date, end_date) AS diff, value FROM test_dates WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, value, datediff('hour', start_date, end_date) - 1 AS adjusted_diff FROM test_dates WHERE datediff('hour', start_date, end_date) > 2;
SELECT AVG(value) AS average_value FROM test_dates GROUP BY datediff('hour', start_date, end_date) HAVING AVG(value) > 75;
SELECT id, datediff('hour', start_date, end_date) AS hour_diff FROM test_dates WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_dates AS inner WHERE inner.value IS NULL AND inner.id = test_dates.id);
SELECT t.id, (datediff('hour', t.start_date, t.end_date) - COALESCE(t.value, 0)) AS net_diff FROM test_dates AS t WHERE t.value IS NULL ORDER BY net_diff DESC;
CREATE TABLE temp_results AS SELECT id, datediff('hour', start_date, end_date) AS hour_diff FROM test_dates WHERE hour_diff > 0;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_results WHERE hour_diff < 24;
